title: `podman run -it fedora /bin/bash` fails on Fedora 41
body:
### Issue Description

I tried  `podman run -it fedora /bin/bash` on Fedora 41 (upgraded Monday night and updated again this morning) and see no output.

I then tried  `podman --log-level=DEBUG run -it fedora /bin/bash` and see the following output [podman.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17576746/podman.log)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -it fedora /bin/bash
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Container is not launched as per:

podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES




### Describe the results you expected

I expected to see a container started.

### podman info output

### Additional information

comments.body: Does `podman ps -a` shows it, your log output clearly shows that the container was started correctly so I assume the bash process exits right away. Can you try using `sleep 10` or another command to check?

podman ps -a shows:

> CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                         COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS                       PORTS                             NAMES
> 2ed05c67106f  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest      /bin/bash             38 minutes ago  Exited (139) 38 minutes ago                                    thirsty_gates
> 2766f72eb600  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest      /bin/bash             38 minutes ago  Exited (139) 38 minutes ago                                    festive_moore
> 279ea7b90f2a  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest      /bin/bash             20 minutes ago  Exited (139) 20 minutes ago                                    sad_moser
> 6
> 

I tried `podman run -d --name sleeptest fedora sleep 10000000` and checked again:

> podman ps -a
> 50ffba0d2f4f  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest      sleep 10000000        About a minute ago  Exited (139) About a minute ago                                    sleeptest

Also from journald output from today is this line:

> Oct 30 14:09:53 scottmarlow audit[207312]: AVC avc:  denied  { map } for  pid=207312 comm="sleep" path="/usr/bin/sleep" dev="fuse" ino=74895428 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c809,c862 tcontext=system_u:object_r:fusefs_t:s0 tclass=file permi>

Looks like an SELinux issue.

Could you make sure your system is up2date


sudo dnf -y update container-selinux

Something might have gone wrong on container-selinux install, so you might want to try

sudo dnf -y reinstall container-selinux

No change from `dnf -y reinstall container-selinux`

> Repositories loaded.
> Package                                                                              Arch             Version                                                                              Repository                                            > Size
> Reinstalling:
>  container-selinux                                                                    noarch            2:2.233.0-1.fc41                                                                      fedora                                             > 68.1 KiB
>   replacing container-selinux                                                        noarch            2:2.233.0-1.fc41                                                                      fedora                                             68.1 KiB

Now run your AVC's though audit2allow 

audit2allow -la

audit2allow -la

> 
> #============= container_t ==============
> 
> #!!!! This avc can be allowed using one of the these booleans:
> #     domain_can_mmap_files, virt_sandbox_use_fusefs
> allow container_t fusefs_t:file map;
> 
> #============= init_t ==============
> allow init_t unlabeled_t:file execute;
> 
> #============= setfiles_t ==============
> allow setfiles_t self:capability2 mac_admin;
sudo setsebool -P virt_sandbox_use_fusefs=1
Isn't there a test suite for things like this in the Fedora release model ?

Output from `setsebool -P -V virt_sandbox_use_fusefs=1`:

> libsepol.context_from_record: type ktlshd_exec_t is not defined
> libsepol.context_from_record: could not create context structure
> libsepol.context_from_string: could not create context structure
> libsepol.sepol_context_to_sid: could not convert system_u:object_r:ktlshd_exec_t:s0 to sid
> invalid context system_u:object_r:ktlshd_exec_t:s0
> libsemanage.semanage_validate_and_compile_fcontexts: setfiles returned error code 255.
> Failed to commit changes to booleans: Success

Output from `getsebool -a | grep virt_sandbox_use_fusefs`
> virt_sandbox_use_fusefs --> on
This looks like issues in selinux-policy or at least whoever is providing ktlshd_t?

> Isn't there a test suite for things like this in the Fedora release model ?

No there are infinite combinations of applications and policies.  This boolean is usually shut off and I have not seen this in years of people using containers. 

Are you using fuse-overlay instead of overlayfs?
Isn't `podman run -it fedora /bin/bash` a simple test case ?
Maybe it could be run with the various configurations...

> Are you using fuse-overlay instead of overlayfs?

Good question, I started with Fedora 9 so anything is possible.  I'm reading a bit more via https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/podman-rootless-overlay but still looking for the how to tell if I am using one or the other and how to switch.
What does podman info show?  You might want to do a `podman system reset`  which will blow away all of your images and containers but will get you to a known good state.

`podman info --debug` output is here [podmaninfo.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17587959/podmaninfo.txt)

I tried `podman system reset -f` as root and no complaints from that but running `podman system reset -f` as non-root gives some complaints:

> WARN[0000] Found incomplete layer "ff2dc218b0847214c5bfbd5a86a6d607a28ab9b8367d753770fb34c46518e1ef", deleting it

>
Should I manually delete (as root) the ^ folders that had permission denied problems?
I have to do that, and then it will work
I mean the `dnf system reset` thing

`dnf system reset` works for me now but I'm still looking to understand how to answer `Are you using fuse-overlay instead of overlayfs?` as the launched VM still exits immediately (`podman run -it fedora /bin/bash`).

Some possible answers:

> podman info | grep -i over
>     capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
>   graphDriverName: overlay
>     Native Overlay Diff: "true"
>   GoVersion: go1.23.2

In case it helps others to see my sestatus:

> sestatus
> SELinux status:                 enabled
> SELinuxfs mount:                /sys/fs/selinux
> SELinux root directory:         /etc/selinux
> Loaded policy name:             targeted
> Current mode:                   enforcing
> Mode from config file:          enforcing
> Policy MLS status:              enabled
> Policy deny_unknown status:     allowed
> Memory protection checking:     actual (secure)
> Max kernel policy version:      33
>

Don't use this workaround but I now have Podman working again temporarily:

Starting Podman container:
> podman run -it fedora /bin/bash
> [root@99f1d9c5f55d /]#

Listing Podman containers:
> podman ps
> CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS         PORTS       NAMES
> 99f1d9c5f55d  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest  /bin/bash   17 seconds ago  Up 18 seconds              mystifying_jones

Temporary workaround of disabling SELinux:
> setenforce Permissive

Enabling SELinux after container is stopped (e.g. you are done with it):
> setenforce Enforcing

Note that enabling SELinux while contain is still running will cause container to exit:
> podman ps -a
> 99f1d9c5f55d  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest  /bin/bash   5 minutes ago   Exited (0) 2 minutes ago                 mystifying_jones

More info but similar to previous post:

> ausearch -m  ALL -ts recent
> ----
> time->Fri Nov  1 09:45:19 2024
> type=AVC msg=audit(1730468719.431:433): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=12371 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c42,c159 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c42,c159 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
> ----
> time->Fri Nov  1 09:45:19 2024
> type=AVC msg=audit(1730468719.431:434): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=12371 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c42,c159 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c42,c159 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
> ----
> time->Fri Nov  1 09:45:19 2024
> type=AVC msg=audit(1730468719.431:435): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=12371 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c42,c159 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c42,c159 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
> ----
> time->Fri Nov  1 09:45:19 2024
> type=AVC msg=audit(1730468719.431:436): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=12371 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c42,c159 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c42,c159 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
> ----
> time->Fri Nov  1 09:45:19 2024
> type=AVC msg=audit(1730468719.432:437): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=12371 comm="bash" path="/usr/lib64/libc.so.6" dev="dm-0" ino=114881106 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c42,c159 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:data_home_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0

I think ran `journalctl -t setroubleshoot` and went to the end of the output which mentioned:
> Nov 01 09:49:15 scottmarlow setroubleshoot[13075]: SELinux is preventing bash from read access on the file /usr/lib64/libc.so.6. For complete SELinux messages run: sealert -l db17fa16-897f-46cb-bd6e-dc92cda4af6f

So, here I am doing that:

> sealert -l db17fa16-897f-46cb-bd6e-dc92cda4af6f
> SELinux is preventing bash from read access on the file /usr/lib64/libc.so.6.
> 
> *****  Plugin restorecon (99.5 confidence) suggests   ************************
> 
> If you want to fix the label. 
> /usr/lib64/libc.so.6 default label should be lib_t.
> Then you can run restorecon. The access attempt may have been stopped due to insufficient permissions to access a parent directory in which case try to change the following command accordingly.
> Do
>  /sbin/restorecon -v /usr/lib64/libc.so.6
> 
> *****  Plugin catchall (1.49 confidence) suggests   **************************
> 
> If you believe that bash should be allowed read access on the libc.so.6 file by default.
> Then you should report this as a bug.
> You can generate a local policy module to allow this access.
> Do
> allow this access for now by executing:
>  ausearch -c 'bash' --raw | audit2allow -M my-bash
>  semodule -X 300 -i my-bash.pp
> 
> 
> Additional Information:
> Source Context                system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c401,c832
> Target Context                unconfined_u:object_r:data_home_t:s0
> Target Objects                /usr/lib64/libc.so.6 [ file ]
> Source                        bash
> Source Path                   bash
> Port                          <Unknown>
> Host                          scottmarlow
> Source RPM Packages           
> Target RPM Packages           glibc-2.40-3.fc41.x86_64
> SELinux Policy RPM            selinux-policy-targeted-41.24-1.fc41.noarch

Their is something very wrong with you container-selinux install, and your selinux-policy install.  The AVCs you are showing should all be allowed, do not keep trying to add new policy modules.

There seems to be two conversations going here. Please open new issues and talk about them separately.
